description: Voice Analysis Module + Critic Loop + Identity Conditioning: Create humanizer/voice_analysis.py with hedge/booster/pronoun/engagement metrics and voice profiling. Create humanizer/critic.py that re-runs the pipeline if AI risk is too high (max 3 retries). Create humanizer/identity.py for academic identity/role conditioning that injects into LLM prompts. Update humanizer/config.py with new settings (critic thresholds, identity defaults, voice marker lists). Update humanizer/stage_llm_rewrite.py to accept and use identity context in prompts. Update humanizer/pipeline.py to integrate the critic loop. Update humanizer/__init__.py exports.
steps: Create humanizer/voice_analysis.py with: (a) measure_hedges() counting hedging words (might, perhaps, seems to, could, arguably, it appears, one might), (b) measure_boosters() counting certainty words (clearly, definitely, certainly, undoubtedly, obviously, precisely), (c) measure_pronouns() counting first-person and inclusive pronouns (I, we, our, us, my), (d) measure_engagement() counting counter-voice markers (however, on the other hand, one might argue, conversely, alternatively, some scholars), (e) compute_enhanced_ttr() building on existing TTR with hapax legomena ratio and moving-average TTR, (f) detect_idiosyncrasies() finding unusual patterns (parenthetical asides, em-dashes, sentence fragments, questions), (g) get_voice_profile(text) returning a dict with all metrics aggregated for display

Create humanizer/critic.py with a CriticLoop class: __init__(self, pipeline_factory, max_retries=3, risk_threshold=40) where pipeline_factory is a callable that creates a pipeline with a given intensity. The run(text) method: (1) runs pipeline.process(text), (2) computes AI risk score on output via text_analysis.compute_ai_risk_score, (3) also runs voice_analysis.get_voice_profile, (4) if risk > threshold and retries < max_retries, increases intensity by 1 (capped at 5) and re-runs, (5) returns dict with final_text, attempts list (each with text, risk_score, voice_profile, intensity_used), and success boolean

Create humanizer/identity.py with an AcademicIdentity dataclass/class: role (str), field (str), institution (str, optional), style_preference (str: formal/semi-formal/conversational). Include a method get_prompt_injection() that returns a string to prepend/append to LLM system prompts describing the identity. Include get_discourse_preferences() returning list of preferred discourse markers based on field/style.

Update humanizer/config.py: Add CRITIC_DEFAULT_THRESHOLD = 40, CRITIC_MAX_RETRIES = 3, VOICE_HEDGES list, VOICE_BOOSTERS list, VOICE_ENGAGEMENT_MARKERS list, ACADEMIC_ROLES list, ACADEMIC_FIELDS list, STYLE_PREFERENCES list

Update humanizer/stage_llm_rewrite.py: Add identity parameter to __init__. Modify _get_pass1_prompt() and _get_pass2_prompt() to incorporate identity.get_prompt_injection() when identity is provided. The identity string should be inserted after the initial persona description.

Update humanizer/pipeline.py: Add identity parameter to HumanizationPipeline.__init__() and pass it through to LLMRewriter. No critic integration in the pipeline class itself - the critic wraps the pipeline externally.
Update humanizer/__init__.py to export VoiceAnalyzer (or get_voice_profile), CriticLoop, AcademicIdentity
acceptance_criteria: humanizer/voice_analysis.py exists with get_voice_profile(text) returning a dict with keys: hedges, boosters, pronouns, engagement, ttr, enhanced_ttr, idiosyncrasies
humanizer/critic.py exists with CriticLoop class that accepts a pipeline factory and runs retry logic
humanizer/identity.py exists with AcademicIdentity class that generates prompt injection strings
LLMRewriter accepts optional identity parameter and incorporates it into prompts
HumanizationPipeline accepts optional identity parameter and passes to LLMRewriter

All new modules are importable without errors: python -c "from humanizer.voice_analysis import get_voice_profile; from humanizer.critic import CriticLoop; from humanizer.identity import AcademicIdentity"
verification: Run: python -c "from humanizer.voice_analysis import get_voice_profile; profile = get_voice_profile('This might perhaps be significant. We clearly observed the results.'); print(profile); assert 'hedges' in profile and 'boosters' in profile"
Run: python -c "from humanizer.critic import CriticLoop; print('CriticLoop imported OK')"
Run: python -c "from humanizer.identity import AcademicIdentity; identity = AcademicIdentity(role='PhD student', field='Computer Science'); print(identity.get_prompt_injection())"

Run: python -c "from humanizer.pipeline import HumanizationPipeline; from humanizer.identity import AcademicIdentity; p = HumanizationPipeline(intensity=3, identity=AcademicIdentity(role='Professor', field='Physics')); print('Pipeline with identity OK')"
findings: All verification steps pass. Voice analysis correctly counts hedges/boosters/pronouns. Identity prompt injection works. Pipeline accepts identity parameter and passes to LLMRewriter. CriticLoop imports cleanly. No pre-existing tests to break.
